# System Foundation Masterplan

Status: active coordination draft
Status-date: 2026-07-20

## Purpose

Coordinate development of the layered AGF–DPA–Kit–ROS system without replacing any repository-local authoritative plan.

## Governing constraints

- each repository must explicitly adopt changes;
- capture is not planning;
- no downstream implementation claim without exact repository evidence;
- no Kit documentation mutation until Mac access permits use of the Kit's own document and planning commands;
- no Comm-SCI migration before a bounded pilot proves the end-to-end model;
- simplification and falsification are required at every stage.

## Stage 1 — AGF system foundation

Deliver:

- layered architecture and authority model;
- common object and relation vocabulary;
- capture, promotion, adoption and evidence boundaries;
- architecture decisions and system-level success/failure criteria.

Exit evidence:

- independent consistency review;
- graph/schema validation;
- no duplicated authority namespace.

## Stage 2 — DPA adoption and projection contracts

Deliver locally in the DPA Lab:

- explicit AGF adoption analysis;
- graph-object and LLM-context projection requirements;
- freshness, provenance and semantic-equivalence rules;
- controlled import implications for the Kit.

This stage must remain subordinate to the current DPA `MASTERPLAN.md` and evidence gates.

## Stage 3 — Kit runtime adoption — BLOCKED UNTIL MAC

Pending local Kit work:

- inspect current main and planning authority with Agentic-Kit commands;
- register the initiative in the established Direction/planning system;
- define bounded slices for capture commands, graph validation, schema registry, projection rendering, adoption records and handoff integration;
- run document lifecycle, registry, protected-diff, tests, gates, PR and handoff workflows.

Hard gate:

`KIT_MAC_AND_NATIVE_COMMANDS_AVAILABLE = true`

No remote prose-only insertion is permitted as a substitute.

## Stage 4 — ROS operating model

Deliver:

- repository roles and responsibilities;
- work-package lifecycle;
- agent-session and handoff model;
- multi-repository coordination;
- conflict, escalation and closeout semantics;
- explicit dependency on Kit runtime rather than duplicate tooling.

## Stage 5 — End-to-end pilot

Select one bounded workflow and demonstrate:

`capture → promotion → requirement → local plan → work package → PR → gate evidence → handoff → context reprojection`

Success criteria:

- exact provenance survives;
- authority remains unambiguous;
- a successor LLM can continue without chat memory;
- no normative dual maintenance;
- measured context is smaller and more relevant than full-repository loading;
- operational cost is proportionate.

## Stage 6 — Application evaluation

Only after pilot adjudication, evaluate Wrapper and Comm-SCI repositories. Each application must adopt, adapt or reject profiles explicitly and retain domain authority.

## Falsification and simplification triggers

Reconsider or simplify the model when:

- graph types cannot be used consistently by independent agents;
- capture becomes an untriaged dumping ground;
- projection maintenance exceeds demonstrated benefit;
- Kit and ROS duplicate runtime responsibilities;
- cross-repository coordination creates a de facto central planning authority;
- a simpler repository-local mechanism provides equivalent continuity and evidence.

## Current next actions

1. Review and adjudicate the AGF foundation branch.
2. Review the DPA integration branch against the DPA bootstrap and masterplan.
3. Develop the ROS operating-model baseline.
4. Keep Kit Stage 3 blocked and visible until Mac access.
